die_in_throes: Tell them the wrench is still in my pocket.
The seal held longer than I did.
I filed the report. It didn't save me.
Hull breach at airlock 3. Casualty confirmed.
The air tasted wrong. I should've said something.
Target down. No vitals. No window.
I kept the light steady. That's all I did.
Don't tell them I was brave. I wasn't.
Oxygen depleted in section 4. Single loss.
My hands were shaking before the shot. After too.
It wasn't the belt. It was the door.
One round. That's what the log will say.
The pipe didn't give. I did.
Neutralized. Time of death logged. Next?
I thought I heard someone. There was no one.
captain_journal: I keep finding his wrench in the wrong pocket. He always kept it on the left.
The mess is quieter. Not because of the chair — because of the timing of the laugh that's gone.
She signed the airlock log every shift even when she wasn't on rotation. I never asked why.
I watched the readout drop. There wasn't time to feel it then.
The deck plate where she stood is warped. I keep stepping around it like it matters.
He always ate the protein last. The tray comes back untouched now — different hands clear it.
I wrote the casualty report before I wrote the log entry. That's the order the desk requires.
The AI logged the death at 03:41. I was awake and didn't go down there.
Her bunk is still made. The fabric is starting to crease in a different pattern.
I keep thinking I see them at the far end of the corridor. It's always someone else.
The scrubber he realigned is still holding. I catch myself touching the housing when I pass.
No one's taken her shift rotation yet. I put off reassigning it again today.
He hummed when he calculated. The bridge is quiet during nav checks now.
I should've checked the suit myself. I didn't.
The ledger has her name now. The ink doesn't look different from everything else.
